title: MATLAB Problems
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems focused on MATLAB applications for signals and systems. Problems involve plotting odd/even portions of signals, computing signal energy via numerical integration, analyzing jump discontinuities, and working with integrals of signals involving unit step and delta functions.

figures.caption: An RLC circuit with a voltage source x(t), a 1 H inductor, a 1/3 H inductor, a 1/2 F capacitor, and a 1 Ω resistor, with mesh currents q1, q2, and q3 defined for circuit analysis.
figures.description: This figure is a circuit diagram, not a graph, so it has no axes. It shows a voltage source labeled x(t) on the left, a 1 H inductor in the left top branch, a 1/3 H inductor in the right top branch, a vertical 1/2 F capacitor connected from the middle node to the bottom reference node, and a 1 Ω resistor on the right side. Arrows labeled q1 and q2 indicate branch currents along the top path, and q3 is marked through the capacitor with polarity signs across it. Students should notice the central node where the two inductors and the capacitor meet, and how the source drives a network containing two inductors, one capacitor, and one resistor for circuit-analysis equations.
